name: Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Inc.
summary: Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (REGN, Health Care) — composite stance CONSTRUCTIVE. Latest reported quarter FY2026 Q1: revenue $3.6B (+19.1% YoY), net margin 20.2%. Valuation 13.0x forward P/E (-4% vs peers). Spot $638.88 (below 200-DMA).
bull: Top-line momentum — revenue grew +19.1% YoY in FY2026 Q1 to $3.6B, with TTM revenue of $14.92B confirming the run-rate. Growth at this pace is unusual for a company of this scale and reflects either share gains, pricing power, or new-product cycles depending on the line-item mix in the Fundamental tab.

Profitability premium — net margin of 20.2% in the latest quarter sits well above the cross-sector ~10–15% benchmark, indicative of either structural pricing power or operating leverage from scale. Expect this to compress only modestly through-cycle.

Range-positioning balanced — at 47% of the 52-week range ($483.07–$812.27), the setup is neither at a momentum-exhaustion ceiling nor at a capitulation floor. Risk/reward into the next catalyst window screens reasonable.

Sell-side target points higher — consensus PT of $740 (vs spot $638.88) implies +15.8% upside. Cross-checked against the peer-median PE FY1 of 13.5x, the discount to fair value looks underwritten by both buy-side and sell-side.

Options market complacent — front-month implied move of just 3.8% suggests the market is not pricing in a binary outcome at the next print. Asymmetric setup if the company delivers a positive surprise — limited downside priced, full upside available.
bear: Margin trajectory deteriorating — net margin compressed -20.2pp over the trailing quarters. This typically precedes a guidance reset by 1–2 quarters as management gets ahead of the cost-curve issue.

Trend rolling over — spot $638.88 sits -7.9% relative to the 200-DMA ($693.36). The break-down of long-term trend often precedes earnings disappointment by 1–2 quarters as smart-money distribution leads the fundamental print.
trigger_up: Revenue growth re-accelerates above 22% YoY on next print — confirms the demand environment hasn't degraded into the back half.
Net margin expands above 21.7% — confirms pricing power and/or cost discipline thesis.
Management raises FY guide on next quarterly call — direct upward revision to consensus.
trigger_down: Revenue growth decelerates below 16% YoY — signals demand has materially softened versus the latest disclosed run-rate.
Net margin compresses below 18.2% — flags either input-cost pressure or competitive dynamics shifting unfavorably.
Management cuts FY guide on next quarterly call — direct downward revision; historically sees 5–15% drawdown on print.
Decisive break below the 52-week low at $483.07 — technical confirmation that the long-term trend has rolled over.
bottom_line: CONSTRUCTIVE. Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (REGN) printed $3.6B in revenue in FY2026 Q1 (+19.1% YoY), net margin of 20.2%. Trading at 13.0x forward P/E versus peer median of 13.5x — broadly in line with the group.Technical regime is impaired: spot $638.88 below the 200-DMA ($693.36, -7.9%) — long-term trend has rolled over.
